event: Divine Healing — And You'll Be Cured of All Diseases
symbols: reaching hands — the divine touch bringing healing, echoing the Creation of Adam
blue and white swirling forms — the spiritual realm and divine energy
cluster of red flowers or wounds — suffering transformed or diseases being lifted
dark ground below — the earthly realm of pain and mortality
flowing robes — the movement and power of the divine presence
description: An expressionist composition dominated by deep blue and white tones. Large swirling figures rendered in bold, scratchy white and blue lines fill the upper portion. One figure at left appears to reach toward another at right in a gesture reminiscent of Michelangelo's Creation of Adam. A cluster of red and pink forms (flowers or organic shapes) appears at upper left. The lower portion is dark, with rough textures suggesting earth or chaos. The overall style is intense and energetic, with heavy impasto-like strokes.
theological_description: This contemporary work reinterprets the theme of divine healing through an expressionist lens. The reaching gesture between figures echoes the Creation of Adam from the Sistine Chapel, but here the context is healing rather than creation — or perhaps both, as healing is a form of re-creation. Jesus' healing ministry was central to His earthly work: 'He went throughout all Galilee... healing every disease and every affliction among the people' (Matt 4:23). The title's promise, 'you'll be cured of all diseases,' resonates with Isaiah's messianic prophecy: 'He took our illnesses and bore our diseases' (Isa 53:4, quoted in Matt 8:17). The Psalms also celebrate divine healing: 'He heals the brokenhearted and binds up their wounds' (Ps 147:3). The intense blue and the reaching gesture convey both the desperation of the sufferer and the overwhelming power of divine compassion.

scripture_refs.reason: Jesus went about healing every disease and affliction — the promise of healing
He took our illnesses and bore our diseases — divine healing through suffering
He heals the brokenhearted and binds up their wounds
He took our illnesses — Matthew's citation of Isaiah fulfilled in Jesus' healings

_meta.title: And you'll be cured of all diseases.. and I'll take care of you
